message:
feat(ondisk,block,alloc,types): on-disk format enhancements, block cache traits, persistent allocator

Why these changes exist:

FrankenFS previously could only *read* ext4 metadata — it parsed group
descriptors, bitmaps, and inodes but had no way to write updated
accounting back to the image.  This commit closes that gap across four
crates so that the higher-level core engine can perform durable,
crash-safe writes in subsequent work.

On-disk format (ffs-ondisk):
- Adds ext4 superblock state flag constants (EXT4_VALID_FS, EXT4_ERROR_FS,
  EXT4_ORPHAN_FS) needed for crash-recovery detection at mount time.
- Implements Ext4GroupDesc::write_to_bytes() — the serialization
  counterpart to the existing parse_from_bytes(), supporting both 32-byte
  and 64-byte descriptor formats, so the allocator can persist updated
  free-block/inode counters.
- Adds stamp_group_desc_checksum() to compute and write CRC32C checksums
  for group descriptors (matching the kernel's metadata_csum feature),
  ensuring written GDT blocks pass fsck verification.
- Roundtrip unit tests validate 32-bit and 64-bit descriptor serialization
  and checksum stamp-then-verify correctness.

Module exports (ffs-ondisk/lib.rs):
- Re-exports the new state flag constants, write_to_bytes, and
  stamp_group_desc_checksum so downstream crates can use them.

Type definitions (ffs-types):
- Adds write_le_u16 and write_le_u32 helper functions for little-endian
  serialization, used by the group descriptor writer.

Block layer (ffs-block):
- Introduces the BlockCache trait (mark_clean, dirty_blocks_oldest_first,
  evict) to give write-back control paths a cache-aware interface beyond
  the basic BlockDevice read/write/sync.
- Adds a DirtyTracker with deterministic age semantics: a logical clock
  (monotonic sequence numbers) tracks when each block was dirtied, enabling
  oldest-first writeback ordering for the upcoming background flusher.
- Extends CacheMetrics with dirty_bytes, oldest_dirty_age_ticks, and a
  dirty_ratio() helper so the MVCC layer can apply backpressure when the
  cache is overloaded.
- Implements a background flush thread (FlushDaemon) that periodically
  writes back the oldest dirty blocks, bounded by configurable dirty-ratio
  high/low watermarks and a maximum dirty age, preventing unbounded dirty
  accumulation during sustained writes.
- Adds tracing instrumentation throughout the cache (debug/trace spans for
  hits, misses, evictions, flush cycles) for operational observability.
- Adds the `tracing` crate dependency to Cargo.toml.

Allocator (ffs-alloc):
- Introduces PersistCtx — a context struct carrying the GDT block location,
  descriptor size, metadata_csum flag, and csum_seed — so allocation
  operations can write updated group descriptors back to disk atomically.
- Adds reserved_blocks_in_group() to compute which relative block offsets
  within a group are metadata (superblock copy, GDT, block/inode bitmaps,
  inode table) and must never be handed out as data blocks.
- Adds persist_group_desc() to read-modify-write a single group descriptor
  entry on disk with optional checksum stamping.
- Implements alloc_blocks_persist() and free_blocks_persist() which extend
  the existing alloc_blocks/free_blocks with reserved-block exclusion and
  automatic GDT writeback, forming the foundation for crash-consistent
  block allocation in the core engine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
